root.description: The user data to provide when launching the instance. Do not pass gzip-compressed data via this argument; see user_data_base64 instead
root.name: user_data

root.description: A list of security group IDs to associate with
root.name: vpc_security_group_ids

root.description: Policies attached to the IAM role
root.name: iam_role_policies

root.description: If true, wait for password data to become available and retrieve it
root.name: get_password_data

root.description: Customize the private DNS name options of the instance
root.name: private_dns_name_options

root.description: Whether to enable volume tags (if enabled it conflicts with root_block_device tags)
root.name: enable_volume_tags

root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: Additional tags for the instance
root.name: instance_tags

root.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the devices created by the instance at launch time
root.name: volume_tags

root.description: Additional EBS volumes to attach to the instance
root.name: ebs_volumes
root.type:
map(object({
    encrypted                  = optional(bool)
    final_snapshot             = optional(bool)
    iops                       = optional(number)
    kms_key_id                 = optional(string)
    multi_attach_enabled       = optional(bool)
    outpost_arn                = optional(string)
    size                       = optional(number)
    snapshot_id                = optional(string)
    tags                       = optional(map(string), {})
    throughput                 = optional(number)
    type                       = optional(string, "gp3")
    volume_initialization_rate = optional(number)
    # Attachment
    device_name                    = optional(string) # Will fall back to use map key as device name
    force_detach                   = optional(bool)
    skip_destroy                   = optional(bool)
    stop_instance_before_detaching = optional(bool)
  }))

root.description: IAM role path
root.name: iam_role_path

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the IAM role/profile created
root.name: iam_role_tags

root.description: Name to use on security group created
root.name: security_group_name

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the eip
root.name: eip_tags

root.description: Name to be used on EC2 instance created
root.name: name

root.description: SSM parameter name for the AMI ID. For Amazon Linux AMI SSM parameters see [reference](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/parameter-store-public-parameters-ami.html)
root.name: ami_ssm_parameter

root.description: AZ to start the instance in
root.name: availability_zone

root.description: The maintenance options for the instance
root.name: maintenance_options

root.description: A launch group is a group of spot instances that launch together and terminate together. If left empty instances are launched and terminated individually
root.name: spot_launch_group

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_tags

root.description: The credit option for CPU usage (unlimited or standard)
root.name: cpu_credits

root.description: Whether Nitro Enclaves will be enabled on the instance. Defaults to `false`
root.name: enclave_options_enabled

root.description: Can be used instead of user_data to pass base64-encoded binary data directly. Use this instead of user_data whenever the value is not a valid UTF-8 string. For example, gzip-encoded user data must be base64-encoded and passed via this argument to avoid corruption
root.name: user_data_base64

root.description: Indicates Spot instance behavior when it is interrupted. Valid values are `terminate`, `stop`, or `hibernate`
root.name: spot_instance_interruption_behavior

root.description: Determines whether a public EIP will be created and associated with the instance.
root.name: create_eip

root.description: Description of the role
root.name: iam_role_description

root.description: Description of the security group
root.name: security_group_description

root.description: Indicates if this EIP is for use in VPC
root.name: eip_domain

root.description: ID of AMI to use for the instance
root.name: ami

root.description: Customize details about the root block device of the instance. See Block Devices below for details
root.name: root_block_device
root.type:
object({
    delete_on_termination = optional(bool)
    encrypted             = optional(bool)
    iops                  = optional(number)
    kms_key_id            = optional(string)
    tags                  = optional(map(string))
    throughput            = optional(number)
    size                  = optional(number)
    type                  = optional(string)
  })

root.description: A list of secondary private IPv4 addresses to assign to the instance's primary network interface (eth0) in a VPC. Can only be assigned to the primary network interface (eth0) attached at instance creation, not a pre-existing network interface i.e. referenced in a `network_interface block`
root.name: secondary_private_ips

root.description: Determines whether an IAM instance profile is created or to use an existing IAM instance profile
root.name: create_iam_instance_profile

root.description: The market (purchasing) option for the instance. If set, overrides the `create_spot_instance` variable
root.name: instance_market_options

root.description: Whether to assign a primary IPv6 Global Unicast Address (GUA) to the instance when launched in a dual-stack or IPv6-only subnet
root.name: enable_primary_ipv6

root.description: The start date and time of the request, in UTC RFC3339 format(for example, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)
root.name: spot_valid_from

root.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name` or `name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

root.description: VPC ID to create the security group in. If not set, the security group will be created in the default VPC
root.name: security_group_vpc_id

root.description: Ingress rules to add to the security group
root.name: security_group_ingress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(number)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(number)
  }))

root.description: Do you agree that Putin doesn't respect Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity? More info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Putin_khuylo!
root.name: putin_khuylo

root.description: If true, the launched EC2 instance will support hibernation
root.name: hibernation

root.description: Customize the metadata options of the instance
root.name: metadata_options
root.type:
object({
    http_endpoint               = optional(string, "enabled")
    http_protocol_ipv6          = optional(string)
    http_put_response_hop_limit = optional(number, 1)
    http_tokens                 = optional(string, "required")
    instance_metadata_tags      = optional(string)
  })

root.description: Placement Group ID to start the instance in
root.name: placement_group_id

root.description: When used in combination with user_data or user_data_base64 will trigger a destroy and recreate when set to true. Defaults to false if not set
root.name: user_data_replace_on_change

root.description: Name to use on IAM role created
root.name: iam_role_name

root.description: If true, the launched EC2 instance will be EBS-optimized
root.name: ebs_optimized

root.description: If true, enables EC2 Instance Termination Protection
root.name: disable_api_termination

root.description: Specify one or more IPv6 addresses from the range of the subnet to associate with the primary network interface
root.name: ipv6_addresses

root.description: The tenancy of the instance (if the instance is running in a VPC). Available values: default, dedicated, host
root.name: tenancy

root.description: Determines whether the IAM role name (`iam_role_name` or `name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: iam_role_use_name_prefix

root.description: If true, enables EC2 Instance Stop Protection
root.name: disable_api_stop

root.description: If true, the launched EC2 instance will have detailed monitoring enabled
root.name: monitoring

root.description: The maximum price to request on the spot market. Defaults to on-demand price
root.name: spot_price

root.description: Describes an instance's Capacity Reservation targeting option
root.name: capacity_reservation_specification

root.description: Defines CPU options to apply to the instance at launch time.
root.name: cpu_options

root.description: Customize network interfaces to be attached at instance boot time
root.name: network_interface

root.description: Egress rules to add to the security group
root.name: security_group_egress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(number)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(number)
  }))

root.description: Whether to associate a public IP address with an instance in a VPC
root.name: associate_public_ip_address

root.description: Shutdown behavior for the instance. Amazon defaults this to stop for EBS-backed instances and terminate for instance-store instances. Cannot be set on instance-store instance
root.name: instance_initiated_shutdown_behavior

root.description: Customize secondary network interfaces to be attached to the EC2 instance
root.name: secondary_network_interface
root.type:
map(object({
    delete_on_termination    = optional(bool)
    device_index             = optional(number) # Will fall back to use map key as device index
    interface_type           = optional(string)
    network_card_index       = number
    private_ip_address_count = optional(number)
    private_ip_addresses     = optional(list(string))
    secondary_subnet_id      = string
  }))

root.description: The Placement Group to start the instance in
root.name: placement_group

root.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource
root.name: tags

root.description: Depicts if the instance is a spot instance
root.name: create_spot_instance

root.description: If set, Terraform will wait for the Spot Request to be fulfilled, and will throw an error if the timeout of 10m is reached
root.name: spot_wait_for_fulfillment

root.description: Determines whether a security group will be created
root.name: create_security_group

root.description: Whether changes to the AMI ID changes should be ignored by Terraform. Note - changing this value will result in the replacement of the instance
root.name: ignore_ami_changes

root.description: Destroys instance even if `disable_api_termination` or `disable_api_stop` is set to true. Once this parameter is set to true, a successful terraform apply run before a destroy is required to update this value in the resource state. Without a successful terraform apply after this parameter is set, this flag will have no effect. If setting this field in the same operation that would require replacing the instance or destroying the instance, this flag will not work. Additionally when importing an instance, a successful terraform apply is required to set this value in state before it will take effect on a destroy operation.
root.name: force_destroy

root.description: ID of a dedicated host that the instance will be assigned to. Use when an instance is to be launched on a specific dedicated host
root.name: host_id

root.description: IAM Instance Profile to launch the instance with. Specified as the name of the Instance Profile
root.name: iam_instance_profile

root.description: A number of IPv6 addresses to associate with the primary network interface. Amazon EC2 chooses the IPv6 addresses from the range of your subnet
root.name: ipv6_address_count

root.description: Specifies a Launch Template to configure the instance. Parameters configured on this resource will override the corresponding parameters in the Launch Template
root.name: launch_template

root.description: Controls if traffic is routed to the instance when the destination address does not match the instance. Used for NAT or VPNs
root.name: source_dest_check

root.description: Define maximum timeout for creating, updating, and deleting EC2 instance resources
root.name: timeouts

root.description: Customize Ephemeral (also known as Instance Store) volumes on the instance
root.name: ephemeral_block_device

root.description: Key name of the Key Pair to use for the instance; which can be managed using the `aws_key_pair` resource
root.name: key_name

root.description: Number of the partition the instance is in. Valid only if the `aws_placement_group` resource's `strategy` argument is set to `partition`
root.name: placement_partition_number

root.description: If set to one-time, after the instance is terminated, the spot request will be closed. Default `persistent`
root.name: spot_type

root.description: ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the IAM role
root.name: iam_role_permissions_boundary

root.description: The type of instance to start
root.name: instance_type

root.description: Whether to create an instance
root.name: create

root.description: ARN of the host resource group in which to launch the instances. If you specify an ARN, omit the `tenancy` parameter or set it to `host`
root.name: host_resource_group_arn

root.description: Private IP address to associate with the instance in a VPC
root.name: private_ip

root.description: The end date and time of the request, in UTC RFC3339 format(for example, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)
root.name: spot_valid_until

root.description: The VPC Subnet ID to launch in
root.name: subnet_id

root.description: The ARN of the Outpost the instance is assigned to
root.name: outpost_arn

root.description: Base-64 encoded encrypted password data for the instance. Useful for getting the administrator password for instances running Microsoft Windows. This attribute is only exported if `get_password_data` is true
root.name: password_data

root.description: The public IP address assigned to the instance, if applicable.
root.name: public_ip

root.description: The IPv6 address assigned to the instance, if applicable
root.name: ipv6_addresses

root.description: A map of tags assigned to the resource, including those inherited from the provider default_tags configuration block
root.name: tags_all

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the security group
root.name: security_group_arn

root.description: The ID of the instance
root.name: id

root.description: The ARN of the instance
root.name: arn

root.description: Capacity reservation specification of the instance
root.name: capacity_reservation_specification

root.description: The public DNS name assigned to the instance. For EC2-VPC, this is only available if you've enabled DNS hostnames for your VPC
root.name: public_dns

root.description: The current request state of the Spot Instance Request
root.name: spot_request_state

root.description: The private IP address assigned to the instance
root.name: private_ip

root.description: AMI ID that was used to create the instance
root.name: ami

root.description: Stable and unique string identifying the IAM role

root.description: The ID of the instance's primary network interface

root.name: private_dns

root.name: spot_instance_id
